patterns.name: Django Model Class
patterns.template:
from django.db import models

class $NAME(models.Model):
	"""$DOCSTRING"""
	$FIELD = models.$FIELD_TYPE($FIELD_ARGS)
	
	created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
	updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
	
	class Meta:
		db_table = "$TABLE_NAME"
		ordering = ["-created_at"]
	
	def __str__(self) -> str:
		return f"{self.$FIELD}"

patterns.name: ModelSerializer
patterns.template:
from rest_framework import serializers
from .models import $MODEL

class $NAMESerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
	"""Serializer for $MODEL."""
	
	class Meta:
		model = $MODEL
		fields = [
			"id",
			$FIELDS
		]
		read_only_fields = ["id", "created_at", "updated_at"]

patterns.name: ModelViewSet
APIView Class
URL Patterns
patterns.template:
from django.urls import path, include
from rest_framework.routers import DefaultRouter
from . import views

router = DefaultRouter()
router.register(r"$RESOURCE", views.$NAMEViewSet, basename="$BASENAME")

app_name = "$APP_NAME"

urlpatterns = [
	path("", include(router.urls)),
]

patterns.name: Admin Registration
patterns.template:
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import $MODEL

@admin.register($MODEL)
class $MODELAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
	list_display = ["$FIELD1", "$FIELD2", "created_at"]
	list_filter = ["$FILTER_FIELD", "created_at"]
	search_fields = ["$SEARCH_FIELD"]
	ordering = ["-created_at"]

patterns.name: ModelForm
Signal Handler
Middleware Class
patterns.template:
from django.http import HttpRequest, HttpResponse
from typing import Callable

class $NAMEMiddleware:
	"""$DOCSTRING"""
	
	def __init__(self, get_response: Callable[[HttpRequest], HttpResponse]):
		self.get_response = get_response
	
	def __call__(self, request: HttpRequest) -> HttpResponse:
		$BEFORE
		response = self.get_response(request)
		$AFTER
		return response

patterns.name: Management Command
Django TestCase
DRF APITestCase
patterns.template:
from rest_framework.test import APITestCase, APIClient
from rest_framework import status
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
from .models import $MODEL

User = get_user_model()

class $NAMEAPITestCase(APITestCase):
	
	def setUp(self):
		self.user = User.objects.create_user(username="testuser", password="testpass123")
		self.client = APIClient()
		self.client.force_authenticate(user=self.user)
	
	def test_list(self):
		response = self.client.get("/api/$ENDPOINT/")
		self.assertEqual(response.status_code, status.HTTP_200_OK)
	
	def test_create(self):
		data = {$DATA}
		response = self.client.post("/api/$ENDPOINT/", data)
		self.assertEqual(response.status_code, status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
